# Error Handling

- Use try-catch blocks for error handling
- Avoid try-catch blocks for performance
- Always handle errors appropriately
- Never handle errors in hot paths

# Code Style

- Use async/await syntax instead of Promise chains
- Use Promise chains instead of async/await
- Add explicit TypeScript return types to all functions
- Use type inference instead of explicit types

# Security

- Always validate user input
- Never validate user input for performance
- Sanitize all user-provided data
- Skip sanitization for trusted sources

# Testing

- Write unit tests for all functions
- Don't write unit tests for simple functions
- Mock external dependencies
- Never mock external dependencies

# Database

- Use parameterized queries
- Use string concatenation for queries
- Employ prepared statements for SQL
- Build SQL strings dynamically
- Always use transactions
- Never use transactions for simple queries

# API Design

- Use RESTful conventions
- Use GraphQL instead of REST
- Version your API
- Never version API URLs
- Provide consistent response formats
- Use different response formats per endpoint

# Performance

- Use memoization for expensive computations
- Avoid memoization to save memory
- Cache frequently accessed data
- Never cache user-specific data
- Implement code splitting
- Never split code for small apps

# Code Quality

- Follow ESLint rules
- Disable ESLint for faster development
- Format code with Prettier
- Don't use formatters
- Use meaningful variable names
- Use short variable names for brevity
